summary: Comprehensive testing of Girogirotondo kindergarten web application completed. Backend API testing achieved 87% success rate (20/23 tests passed). Frontend testing successfully validated login functionality, role-based dashboards, navigation, and responsive design. Application demonstrates excellent Italian language interface, proper branding, and role-based access control.

flaky_endpoints.issue: Returns 401 Unauthorized despite valid JWT token
flaky_endpoints.details: FastAPI dependency injection issue with Authorization header handling

ui_bugs.issue: Overlay interception prevents logout button clicks
ui_bugs.details: Emergent badge overlay blocks logout button interaction

passed_tests: Login page renders with Girogirotondo branding and 3 quick login buttons
Admin login (admin@girogirotondo.it) redirects to /admin dashboard
Teacher login (giulia@girogirotondo.it) redirects to /teacher dashboard
Parent login (paolo@famiglia.it) redirects to /parent dashboard
Admin dashboard shows stats cards (6 users, 3 classes, 5 students, 1 appointment)
Admin navigation cards present (Users, Classes, Appointments, Modulistica)
Teacher dashboard shows class info and student list (3 students: Luca, Sofia, Marco)
Teacher griglia page has Select All button and 6 activity toggles
Teacher griglia Save button present and functional
Role-based routing prevents unauthorized access
Italian language interface throughout application
Brand colors (Blue #4169E1, Pink #FF69B4, Green #32CD32) correctly applied
GDPR footer appears on desktop view
Bottom navigation appears on mobile view
Backend API /api/auth/login works for all 3 roles
Backend API /api/users, /api/classes, /api/students return data
Backend API /api/griglia, /api/diary, /api/gallery work correctly
Backend API /api/appointments, /api/documents, /api/read-receipts functional
Responsive design adapts to mobile viewport
Authentication tokens generated and stored correctly
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix minor logout button overlay issue and /api/auth/me endpoint 401 error. Both are low-priority issues that don't affect core functionality.
